assertions.description: Math.sumPrecise is exposed on the global Math object.
Math.sumPrecise([0.1, 0.2]) returns 0.30000000000000004 because the input Number values are already binary floats and the exact mathematical sum is rounded once at the end.
Math.sumPrecise preserves tiny finite terms that naive addition loses, such as [1e20, 0.1, -1e20] returning 0.1.
An empty iterable passed to Math.sumPrecise returns -0.
Math.sumPrecise throws a TypeError if any iterable element is not a Number.
assertions.test: (()=>{if(typeof Math.sumPrecise!=='function')return false;try{Math.sumPrecise(['1']);return false;}catch(e){return e instanceof TypeError;}})()

assertions.description: Math.sumPrecise throws a TypeError if the iterable contains a BigInt.
assertions.test: (()=>{if(typeof Math.sumPrecise!=='function')return false;try{Math.sumPrecise([1n]);return false;}catch(e){return e instanceof TypeError;}})()

assertions.description: Math.sumPrecise propagates NaN if any input value is NaN.
Math.sumPrecise returns NaN when both +Infinity and -Infinity are present.
